steps.title: Identify the defining claim
steps.content:
The passage opens noting Blake championed Avery's work, then narrows to his praise for Little, Big in an afterword.

The key detail: after explaining Keller's theory (works are either naive OR sentimental), the text immediately asserts that Little, Big “demonstrates that a work can be both.” This blend is what makes the novel special—and implicitly, what impresses Blake.

steps.title: Match to the answer
steps.content:
The matching choice directly matches the text's purpose: it presents the specific reason why a literary critic (Blake) is impressed by a particular novel (Little, Big)—the novel achieves a rare blend of two modes Keller thought were distinct.

The other choices either describe a comparison the text doesn't make, reverse the text's argument about categories, or invent authorial inspiration details.

B is correct.
